summary: Iteration-7 (LeadOrbit chatbot flow engine) full backend + frontend verification. 22/22 NEW pytest cases PASS in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration7_chatflows.py; iter4 + iter5 regression also clean (37/37 PASS). Frontend /chatflows page verified end-to-end via Playwright as admin: nav-chatflows → flows-list → new-flow-btn + new-flow-name-input + create-flow-submit-btn → flow-editor → new-node-btn / NewNodeModal (name/type/body/start/submit) → NodeEditor (name/header/body/footer/start) → add-option-btn + per-row option-id/option-label/option-next-select → save-node-btn → flow-test-phone + flow-test-send-btn ALL render and function. Cleaned up the test flow + re-activated the seeded 'Sales Qualification' flow at the end.

minor.issue: When _build_interactive_payload throws (e.g. button >3 options, list with 0 options), the endpoint returns HTTP 200 with body {error: '...'} instead of 4xx. Functionally it surfaces the failure but UX-wise frontend cannot distinguish from a real send: consider returning 400 with structured detail.

minor.endpoint: POST /api/chatflows/{id}/start (text node, lead exists, no inbound)
minor.issue: Confirmed working — text nodes still send (status='sent'/'sent_mock') even when last_user_message_at is unset. Only button/list nodes are gated by 24h. This matches the spec; no fix needed.

ui_bugs.issue: Hydration warning: <button data-testid=flow-row-...> contains nested <button data-testid=toggle-active-...> + <button data-testid=delete-flow-...>. React logs 'In HTML, <button> cannot be a descendant of <button>'. Replace the outer <button> with a <div role="button" tabIndex=0 onClick=...> or move the inner buttons out of the click target. Functionally still works but produces console errors and is invalid HTML.

action_items: Fix nested <button> in ChatFlows.jsx flow-row (replace outer button with div+role=button) to clear React hydration warning.

Optional: change /api/chatflows/{id}/start to return HTTP 400 with structured detail when _build_interactive_payload throws (>3 buttons, empty list options). Currently returns 200 + {error:''} — works but inconsistent with other 4xx contracts.
Optional: split server.py — now 3406 lines. The chatflow engine alone is ~370 lines (2282–2650) and is a clean candidate for /app/backend/chatflows.py.
critical_code_review_comments: API version IS dynamic — wa_send_interactive uses cfg['api_version'] (line 2340). Source-grep confirms zero hardcoded 'v19.0' / 'v20.0' / 'v21.0' literals outside the WHATSAPP_API_VERSION env default at line 49. Verified by automated test test_no_hardcoded_v_prefixed_versions_in_server.
Cascade on flow delete is correct: delete_chatflow loops nodes → bulk-deletes options → deletes nodes → deletes flow.

Node delete correctly clears next_node_id refs in *other* options via update_many({next_node_id: node_id}, {$set: {next_node_id: None}}) (line 2604) — verified by test_node_delete_clears_next_node_refs.

handle_flow_inbound covers all four spec branches: (a) session present + matching option → advance, (b) session present + unknown option → keep session (returns {status:'no_match'}), (c) no session + matching start-option → auto-start advance, (d) no session + no flow → {status:'no_active_flow'}. All four paths are covered by tests.
_is_within_24h_window uses datetime.fromisoformat with Z→+00:00 normalization and tz-aware comparison — robust.
send_flow_message persists flow_id + flow_node_id on outbound message and upserts chat_sessions atomically on (sent|sent_mock) — correct ordering: insert message → set has_whatsapp → upsert session.
Minor: button title is truncated to 20 chars and list row title to 24 chars (Meta limits) — matches spec; section title also clipped to 24.
Frontend NodeEditor save logic is atomic (PATCH node THEN PUT options THEN reload) — race-free since both are admin-only single-user edits.
ChatFlows.jsx outer flow-row uses <button> wrapping inner action <button>s — invalid HTML, see frontend_issues.ui_bugs.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on testid coverage and create→edit→options→save→test-send flow exercised manually via Playwright

test_credentials: admin/Admin@123 (verified working). ravi/Exec@123 used for 403 RBAC checks.
seed_data_creation: Test fixtures auto-create then auto-delete TEST_IT7_* flows + nodes + options + a small number of TEST_IT7_BTN_* / TEST_IT7_RR-style leads via existing /api/leads. Module-scoped autouse fixture _cleanup deletes every flow it created. After the run only the seeded 'Sales Qualification' flow remains (re-activated post-test).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run via: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://crm-lead-mgmt-3.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration7_chatflows.py /app/backend/tests/test_iteration5.py /app/backend/tests/test_iteration4.py -v` (env var is needed because conftest.py rstrips it). The seeded 'Sales Qualification' flow id 96806721-76bc-46a1-8d4f-506e8682abce is currently is_active=True; do not delete it. Webhook simulate endpoint /api/webhooks/whatsapp/_debug/simulate is admin-only; use it to open the 24h window for any test phone before button/list start. Real WA creds are configured so wa_send_interactive returns status='sent' with a wamid (mock path 'sent_mock' only kicks in if creds are cleared).
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs blocking iter-7 acceptance. One MEDIUM-priority React hydration warning logged (nested <button> inside flow-row); functionally inert but should be fixed. One minor API-shape comment: build-payload validation errors come back as HTTP 200 + {error} from /chatflows/{id}/start instead of 4xx — works but inconsistent.
